environment.description: Choose the environment you'd like to test on, either Sandbox (uses testing API tokens) `api-sandbox` or Production (uses live API tokens) `api`

bearerToken.description: Enter the API token that is provided by Tamara to be sent in Authorization header.

post.description:
This endpoint facilitates the creation of a checkout session, where all payment information is sent within the request to enable customer payments via Tamara. The response will include `order_id`, `checkout_id` , `status` and `checkout_url`.

Please store the `order_id` in your DBs to fetch the information about the order later and direct the customer to the `checkout_url` to seamlessly conclude the transaction through Tamara.

total_amount.description: Total amount to be charged to consumer.

shipping_amount.description: Total amount for the shipping of the order.

tax_amount.description: Total amount of taxes, if additionally applied.

order_reference_id.description: Unique order ID from the merchant's side, which will be used for settlement and reporting purposes. Can be modified after the checkout session is created.

order_number.description: The order number from the merchant side, this will be used for communication with the customer. If this value is not passed, the order_number will take the order_reference_id value.

discount.description: This object is used to mention any customer-specific discount/voucher code being used for this specific order, but not to be used for site-wide discounts.

items.description: An array of objects to detail the items in this order as seperate objects for each item.

quantity.description: The quantity being purchased for this specific item.

reference_id.description: The unique id of the item from merchant's side

item_url.description: URL of the item from merchant's website. **`<= 1024 characters`**

image_url.description:
URL to an image of the product that can be later displayed to the customer. **`<= 1024 characters`**
**Size** = 2-3 MB maximum
**Resolution** WxH = 1024xY (the Y height of image should be small).

merchant_url.description: This object includes all the redirect URLs that the customer will be redirected to from the Tamara checkout page in different cases.

billing_address.description: The customer's billing address, if any.

platform.description: Mentions the platform where the Tamara order is being initiated from (Mostly used by our e-commerce plugins) but can also be used by direct integrations.

is_mobile.description: To identify mobile users of your store.

locale.description: Display language for Tamara checkout page. Language to be defined by the merchant following RFC 1766, e.g en_US or ar_SA. Default is set to Arabic if not passed and customer is new. If customer already exists and locale is not passed then customer's preference will be taken into account.

risk_assessment.description: Risk assessment info from the merchant side

is_premium_customer.description: Is VIP or any kind of premium customer tiers like Platinum, Gold...etc

is_existing_customer.description: Customer's account was created in the past, not on the same day as the order is created.

is_guest_user.description: Is the customer performing guest checkout on your store.

account_creation_date.description: Date of customer registration of their account (format dd-mm-yyyy)

platform_account_creation_date.description: Date of customer registration of their account with the platform like Salla (format dd-mm-yyyy)

date_of_first_transaction.description: Date of consumer’s first transaction. (format dd-mm-yyyy)

is_card_on_file.description: Does customer have any saved cards on his account?

is_COD_customer.description: Does customer only use Cash on Delivery as their payment method previously?

has_delivered_order.description: Does the customer have successfully delivered orders in the past?

is_phone_verified.description: Is the customer's registered phone verified?

is_fraudulent_customer.description: Does customer have any history of fraudulent activity in your records like false chargebacks, stolen card usage etc.?

total_ltv.description: Lifetime value. The total amount (in local currency) the consumer has ordered, excluding canceled, rejected, or refunded transactions. Also excluding **Tamara** payments.

total_order_count.description: Number of orders the customer has made at your store since creating an account, excluding canceled, rejected, or refunded transactions. Also excluding **Tamara** payments.

order_amount_last3months.description: Amount the consumer spent in the last 3 months, excluding canceled, rejected, or refunded transactions. Also excluding **Tamara** payments.

order_count_last3months.description: Number of orders the consumer placed in the last 3 months, excluding canceled, rejected, or refunded transactions. Also excluding **Tamara** payments.

last_order_date.description: Date on which the consumer made their last purchase, excluding **Tamara** payments (format dd-mm-yyyy)

last_order_amount.description: Amount (in local currency) of the last order, excluding **Tamara** payments.

reward_program_enrolled.description: Is customer enrolled in any of your reward program?

reward_program_points.description: Number of reward points earned by the customer so far.

expires_in_minutes.description: Order expiry time in minutes, min 5 minutes, max 1440 (one day). By default this key will be ignored, and default value of 30 mins is used, **Please contact our support team to enable this feature**.

additional_data.description: Any additional order data information from the merchant side

delivery_method.description: the delivery method selected by the customer e.g. home delivery, or click and collect

pickup_store.description: the collection point selected by the user if his order is for store collection

store_code.description: The unique store code/name from which request is called

vendor_amount.description: The amount to be settled to vendor

merchant_settlement_amount.description: The amount to be settled to merchant

example.message: Unsupported country-currency combination. Please reach out to your account manager for support

get.summary: Get Order Details by Tamara's order_id
get.description: Fetch all the order details available on **Tamara's** side using the unique `order_id` that is issued by **Tamara** for every order whether online or in-store.

parameters.name: order_id
parameters.description: Unique Tamara `order_id` from the response of the creation of the checkout session whether online or in-store.

post.description: This endpoint plays a crucial role in the online checkout flow and should be executed upon receipt of the `approved` status webook event from **Tamara**. Its primary function is to update the order status to `authorised` ensuring status synchronization and smooth progression of the online order flow.

parameters.name: order_id
parameters.description: Unique Tamara `order_id`, obtained from the response of create checkout/in-store checkout session endpoint.

post.description: This endpoint is requested to perform a full or partial capture of the order, confirming the fulfillment or shipment of the items to the customer. The order status value will be `fully_captured` or `partially_captured` based on the total amount value sent in the request.

order_id.description: Unique Tamara `order_id`, obtained from the response of create checkout/in-store checkout session endpoint.

total_amount.description: Total amount to be captured out of the original order total.

shipping_company.description: Name of the shipping company. Limited to 100 characters.

tracking_number.description: Tracking number of shipment. Limited to 100 characters.

tracking_url.description: URL where the customer can track their shipment. `<=1024 characters`.

items.description: An array of objects to detail the items in this order as seperate objects for each item.

quantity.description: The quantity being fulfilled for this specific item.

reference_id.description: The unique id of the item from merchant's side

item_url.description: URL of the item from merchant's website. **`<= 1024 characters`**

image_url.description:
URL to an image of the product that can be later displayed to the customer. **`<= 1024 characters`**
**Size** = 2-3 MB maximum
**Resolution** WxH = 1024xY (the Y height of image should be small).

shipping_amount.description: Total amount for the shipping of the order.

tax_amount.description: Total amount of taxes, if additionally applied.

post.description: This endpoint is requested to cancel or update the total order amount while order status on `authorised` (before the order is captured or shipping process is done). The order status value will be `canceled` or `updated` based on the modification applied to the total amount.

total_amount.description: Total amount to be charged back to consumer.

shipping_amount.description: Total amount for the shipping of the order.

tax_amount.description: Total amount of taxes, if additionally applied.

items.description: An array of objects to detail the items in this order as seperate objects for each item.

quantity.description: How many of this specific item is being purchased

reference_id.description: The unique id of the item from merchant's side

item_url.description: URL of the item from merchant's website. **`<= 1024 characters`**

image_url.description:
URL to an image of the product that can be later displayed to the customer. **`<= 1024 characters`**
**Size** = 2-3 MB maximum
**Resolution** WxH = 1024xY (the Y height of image should be small).

parameters.name: order_id
parameters.description: Unique Tamara `order_id`, obtained from the response of create checkout/in-store checkout session endpoint.

post.description: This API is to be used to process refunds for captured orders.

total_amount.description: Total amount to be refunded to consumer, not including any discount amount.

comment.description: Notes or comments as a reference point that will be added to this order's transaction history.

merchant_refund_id.description: Used to input the merchant's own internal refund ID, if any, to be stored on the refund request and order details.

parameters.name: order_id
parameters.description: Unique Tamara `order_id` from the response of the creation of the checkout session whether online or in-store.

post.description: This API endpoint provides the functionality to void any In-Store checkout session. This is particularly useful in-store when customers need to make any changes to their orders being paid with Tamara.

parameters.name: order_id
parameters.description: Unique Tamara `order_id`, obtained from the response of create checkout/in-store checkout session endpoint.

parameters.name: store_code
parameters.description: The unique store code/name from which request is called

parameters.name: checkout_id
parameters.description: Unique Tamara `checkout_id`, obtained from the response of create checkout/in-store checkout session endpoint.

put.description: Update `order_reference_id` value that is stored at **Tamara's** side at any point after the original checkout session has been created.

value.message: Order reference id was updated successfully

parameters.name: order_id
parameters.description: Unique Tamara `order_id` from the response of the creation of the checkout session whether online or in-store.

post.description: This endpoint helps you to check whether Tamara treats a customer as eligible or not, based on active decline records we hold for that customer.
